edits.description: PR-mined: real upstream range v1.4.0..v1.4.2 (privacy-friendly-2048), specifically the SettingsActivity super-qualifier cleanup + secusoLogoDrawable theme wiring (commit range collapsed to its net diff; version-bump-only commits in between excluded). Adds a new declare-styleable attr + wires AppTheme's colorScheme item to a NEW drawable resource (secuso_logo_black.xml, secuso_logo_white.xml -- real vendored content, not authored) while also touching a Java class (SettingsActivity's onCreate drops an unneeded super. qualifier). 'add' in editClass because two brand-new drawable resource NAMES are introduced, needing R regen. recompiledClasses upper bound is widened past SettingsActivity(+nested fragment) to 6: adding new drawable IDs shifts other R.* int constants, so any class with an inlined R reference recompiles too -- the README's documented 'R-id churn' caveat, reproduced here on real content rather than synthetic.

Authored (not PR-mined): real post-pin upstream history for 2048 is thin (only a versionCode-only commit after v1.4.2, already used as edit 01's source range). Clarifies the 'undo' button label from 'Undo' to 'Undo Last Move'. No new string names, resources-only relink.

Authored (not PR-mined; see edit 02's note on thin post-pin history). FirstLaunchManager.initFirstTimeLaunch() actually clears the first-time-launch flag now (the real upstream method body is an empty if-block, a genuine dead-code spot in the real app -- this edit fills it in with the plausible fix plus a marker).

Adding a field to a data class changes its ABI (copy/equals/hashCode/toString), which correctly ripples to JComposeCoGoHostActivity -- the harness's own consumer that constructs HomeUiState() directly -- so 3 classes recompile (HomeViewModel + HomeUiState from the edited file, plus the ABI-dependent HostActivity), not 1-2.

PR-mined (Neamar/KISS, "static StringNormalizer.Result", 71ad3234, 2025-10-20): adds a static Result.EMPTY constant and an early-return for empty/null input in normalizeWithResult -- a real recent perf/null-safety improvement, applied cumulatively on edit01.

edits.description: NodeRenderer.render() widens its return type from String to CharSequence. Only the .java file is touched, but TreeNode.describe() infers its return type from that call, so TreeNode's own ABI changes and every caller of describe() (MainActivity) plus the Java override (JavaLeafNode, which now needs a covariant bridge) must be recompiled. If the Kotlin side were left stale it would still call render()Ljava/lang/String; - a NoSuchMethodError at run time - so this edit is the never-stale check for the Java->Kotlin direction of the cycle.

edits.description: PR-mined: real upstream commit fd799d4ef9a53be68c3d3323710071cf2adb936d ('Translated using Weblate (English)', privacy-friendly-ruler). Retranslates a couple of strings (okay -> Ok; viewhelp picks up a stray French string, a genuine upstream Weblate mistranslation bug) plus incidental whitespace/XML-declaration normalization. No new string names, so no R regen needed -- resources-only relink.

PR-mined: real upstream commit 37b67200487beee92bf018c76cd6e43e518ad30d ('Translated using Weblate (German)', privacy-friendly-ruler). Retranslates pref_rightruler_default/pref_anglemeasure_default and a few other German strings; whitespace normalization. Applied as a full-file replacement (not a .patch) because vendor.json deliberately vendors two files both basenamed strings.xml (default + values-de) and fetch_vendored.py's patch step keys its base-file lookup on basename alone -- see vendor.json's note. No new string names, resources-only relink.

PR-mined: real upstream commit 51ffdbe27050d624f9b75032d429a60176255fd8 ('Added translation using Weblate (French)', privacy-friendly-ruler). Adds a brand-new values-fr/strings.xml -- but the real commit's file is an empty placeholder (<resources></resources>, no <string> entries yet), so it introduces zero new R.string names despite being a whole new resource file. editClass deliberately omits 'add' (the README's R-regen trigger substring): no new resource NAMES exist to regenerate R for, only a new (empty) qualifier directory.

edits.description: PR-mined: modeled after the real upstream commit 89fe30473e88a1adf8354c6b607c2c0515d8cfba 'docs: update the doc of IntPair' (Rosemoe, 2025-12-13 -- a direct maintainer commit, not routed through a PR; verified via GitHub's commits/{sha}/pulls API returning no associated PR), which documented/grew IntPair's small pure-function helper set (packIntFloat/getSecondAsFloat). That exact commit is already baked into our pinned commit (43f7e8e2981...), so a literal forward-patch of its diff is a no-op against this baseline. This edit reproduces the same real transformation (growing IntPair's helper set with a small doc'd pure function) with the natural next helper: swap().

assertions.name: behavioral_marker
assertions.detail: marker 'quick build reloaded IntPair.swap' found in recompiled class bytes

edits.description: PR-mined: modeled after the real upstream commit 192c9356991df297a0c02f9d1c85a24e4022aacc 'feat(editor): no longer use enforced LTR base direction in editor' (Rosemoe, 2025-11-10), which moved TextBidi.getDirections off a hardcoded Bidi.DIRECTION_LEFT_TO_RIGHT onto Bidi.DIRECTION_DEFAULT_LEFT_TO_RIGHT. That commit is already baked into our pinned commit (TextBidi.java already uses DIRECTION_DEFAULT_LEFT_TO_RIGHT), so a literal forward-patch of its diff is a no-op. This edit continues the same real spirit ('stop enforcing a single base direction') with the natural next step: an overload that takes an explicit Bidi direction flag, with the existing single-arg method delegating to it.

assertions.name: behavioral_marker
assertions.detail: marker 'quick build reloaded TextBidi.getDirections(text, bidiFlags)' found in recompiled class bytes

edits.description: PR-mined: modeled after the real upstream commit 10370f833f496363d44a4d76380e29bd1e7e77e2 'perf(editor): avoid copying when create String in Latin1 implementation' (Rosemoe, 2026-03-03), which changed StringLatin1.newString() from a char[]-roundtrip to a direct new String(bytes, offset, length, ISO_8859_1) decode. That commit is already baked into our pinned commit's newString() (visible a few lines below this edit's change), so a literal forward-patch of its diff is a no-op. This edit applies the SAME real perf pattern to the sibling method appendTo(), which still had the pre-fix per-char loop.

assertions.name: behavioral_marker
assertions.detail: marker 'quick build reloaded StringLatin1.appendTo perf path' found in recompiled class bytes

edits.description: PR-mined: modeled after the real merged StreetComplete PR #6318 'util: handle duplicate elements in Collection.containsExactlyInAnyOrder()' (2025-08-13), which fixed containsExactlyInAnyOrder to use a count-map instead of a naive containsAll check. That fix is already baked into our pinned commit (26c673030a09..., tag v63.2), so a literal forward-patch of the PR's own diff is a no-op against this baseline. This edit reuses the same count-map idiom for a new, related helper: mostCommonOrNull().

assertions.name: behavioral_marker
assertions.detail: marker 'quick build reloaded Collections.mostCommonOrNull' found in recompiled class bytes

edits.description: PR-mined: modeled after the real merged StreetComplete PR #6311 'fix: correct Vector3d division and subtraction operations' (2025-06-06), which fixed copy-paste bugs in Vector3d's div()/minus() operators (they referenced the wrong component). That fix is already baked into our pinned commit (div/minus already use x/y/z correctly), so a literal forward-patch of the PR's own diff is a no-op against this baseline. This edit continues the same 'make Vector3d's per-component operators complete' spirit by adding destructuring component1/2/3() operators.

assertions.name: behavioral_marker
assertions.detail: marker "quick build reloaded Vector3d's destructuring operators" found in recompiled class bytes

edits.description: PR-mined: modeled after the real merged StreetComplete PR #6307 'Fix angle normalization with non-zero startAt parameter' (2025-06-06), which fixed the normalizeDegrees/normalizeRadians formulas and their docs. That fix is already baked into our pinned commit, so a literal forward-patch of the PR's own diff is a no-op against this baseline. This edit continues the same file's real overload asymmetry: normalizeDegrees has both Double and Float overloads, but normalizeRadians only had Double -- this adds the missing Float overload.

edits.description: PR-mined: real upstream commit 85895f3 ('[fix] changes foreground service type to special use'). Switches the on-Android-14+ ServiceCompat.startForeground() call from FOREGROUND_SERVICE_TYPE_SHORT_SERVICE to FOREGROUND_SERVICE_TYPE_SPECIAL_USE, matching the manifest's specialUse declaration. behavioralMarker is null: both constants are javac-inlined ints (ServiceInfo.FOREGROUND_SERVICE_TYPE_* are compile-time constants), so neither name survives into the recompiled class bytes -- same class of issue as the README's R-id-churn note, just via an SDK constant instead of an R field.

Authored edit (not PR-mined -- see vendor.json for why) against the real, vendored AlarmMgr.kt: adds a hasPendingAlarm() query method plus a log line, in the same spirit as the real alarm-scheduling API it sits beside.

assertions.name: behavioral_marker
assertions.detail: marker 'Checked pending alarm state via hasPendingAlarm()' found in recompiled class bytes
